reason: Gateway retry and circuit changes must follow the backend pre-development and quality checklist.
Preserve provider attempt budgets, configured transient reservations, strict routes, and circuit accounting.
The rule contract crosses Rust settings, generated bindings, frontend adapters, UI, and runtime.
Keep generic failover decisions, attempt reasons, route projection, and auth-body privacy stable.
Global policy writes must preserve field ownership, locked validation, and rollback semantics.
Provider share v1/v2 must remain strict, deterministic, bounded, and secret-safe.
Use the complete data-flow and boundary checklist for the new rule payload.
Local evidence comparing current retry behavior with sub2api and identifying all migration surfaces.
